dailyJudgementSnippet: OpenAI edged the day because HN commenters gave GPT 5.4 and Codex concrete credit for useful model behavior in security work and browser-game automation, while its one direct company story mostly read as a routine acquihire with some ecosystem concern rather than a backlash event [E2][E3][E4].
winnerExplanation: OpenAI finished first because its relevant mentions were net favorable: GPT 5.4 was explicitly praised in the Mythos discussion, Codex got a tangible success anecdote, and the Cirrus Labs post was discussed more as a neutral staffing move than as a reputational hit. No other tracked provider combined as much positive capability signal with comparable front-page coverage [E2][E3][E4].

openai.judgementSnippet: GPT 5.4 and Codex were cited as effective in open-ended bug hunting and browser-game automation, while the Cirrus Labs story was treated mainly as a talent acquisition for agentic tooling and only secondarily as an ecosystem loss from the shutdown of Cirrus CI [E2][E3][E4].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Mythos drew repeated pushback for apples-to-oranges methodology, heavy prompting/scaffolding, and benchmark-trust concerns, although Claude did pick up one smaller positive anecdote for one-shotting a git diff-driver integration [E1][E5][E6].

evidence.summary: In the Mythos discussion, top comments argued Anthropic's showcased results were not apples-to-apples because the comparison isolated vulnerable code and gave stronger hints than a real full-codebase search.

The same Mythos thread also contained explicit praise for GPT 5.4, with commenters saying weaker models had failed for them while GPT 5.4 and other frontier models were effective for open-ended vulnerability hunting.
A top comment on the Starfling game said Codex 5.4 learned how to play after several attempts and then wrote an autoplay script that reached the current high score.
Comments on 'Cirrus Labs to join OpenAI' framed it as a talent-focused acquihire for agentic engineering, while also lamenting the shutdown of Cirrus CI and possible effects on open-source users.

In the benchmark-exploit thread, a top comment speculated that Anthropic may have delayed releasing Mythos because the real system could disappoint relative to benchmark scores, reinforcing skepticism around its benchmark framing.
A top comment on the git diff-driver post said Claude handled the integration in one shot, providing a compact but direct positive anecdote about model usefulness.
